text:
There once was a farmer who noticed that birds often hung around a particular oak tree near his farmhouse. Curious about these visitors, he climbed the tree one day to investigate.

What he found was a family of extraordinary owls, feeding on a gigantic moth that seemed almost magical. The owl parents taught their young how to hunt these huge moths by guiding them with their owlish echolocation.

Months later, the tree was covered in these giant moth pupae. The farmer organized a festival to admire the moths, inviting everyone who loved big bugs. The owl parents were proud.

Years went by, and the moths layed huge eggs. When those eggs hatched, the owlets grew hugemothlings. These giant moths would swoop down and pluck insects from trees!

The farmer started collecting these moths to keep in a special cage he called the Moth Tower. Guests would pay a lot of money to see them.
